title: Informed Consent for Medical Treatment
description: A legal consent document outlining risks, benefits, and alternative pathways for patients prior to undergoing medical or cosmetic treatments.
whatsCovered: Detailed description of the proposed medical treatment
Acknowledgement of clinical risks and complications
Patient declaration of understanding and voluntary consent
whenToUse: Use this form in a clinic, hospital, or wellness setting to secure a patient's informed, written consent prior to administering treatment, surgery, or therapy.

fields.label: Proposed Treatment Description
fields.placeholder: Describe the medical, dental, or aesthetic procedure to be performed...

clauses.heading: INFORMED CONSENT AND DISCLOSURE
clauses.body: This Informed Consent is signed on {effective_date} by {patient_name}, DOB: {patient_dob} (the "Patient"), in favor of {provider_name} (the "Provider") regarding the proposed treatment.

clauses.heading: 1. Description of Treatment
clauses.body: The Patient has requested and hereby consents to the following medical or wellness procedure: {treatment_desc}. The Provider has explained the nature and purpose of this procedure to the Patient.

clauses.heading: 2. Clinical Risks & Complications
clauses.body: Patient understands that all medical treatments carry inherent risks, including infection, swelling, bruising, allergic reactions, and failure to achieve desired clinical results. Patient acknowledges that no guarantees have been made.

clauses.heading: 3. Alternatives Explained
clauses.body: The Provider has discussed reasonable alternative treatments, including the option of receiving no treatment at all, and Patient has chosen to proceed with the specified procedure.

clauses.heading: 4. Clinical Photography Consent
clauses.body: Patient consents to the taking of clinical photographs of the treatment area for medical records, tracking progress, and clinical study display (excluding public advertising).

clauses.heading: 5. Governing Law
clauses.body: This consent document shall be interpreted and governed under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: What is 'informed consent'?
faq.answer: Informed consent means you have been fully educated on the procedure, risks, benefits, and alternatives so you can make an educated choice about your healthcare.

faq.question: Can I revoke my consent before treatment?
faq.answer: Yes. You have the right to change your mind and revoke your consent at any time prior to the commencement of the procedure.
